title: Repair legacy ledger audit failures and document prevention rules
summary: Analyze the existing audit:harness failures in legacy task ledgers, apply append-only or minimally scoped repairs, and update guide documentation so the same ledger/session logging mistakes are less likely to recur.

include: logs/tasks/TASK-20260426-062.jsonl through affected legacy ledgers reported by audit:harness
session log artifacts needed to satisfy strict-era completion gates
work-history or related operational guide documentation
exclude: rewriting completed historical task events except where required to repair invalid JSON

success_criteria: Root causes for each audit:harness failure are identified and recorded in the session/report.
All affected legacy ledger failures are repaired without creating new incomplete tasks.
Guide documentation includes a concrete prevention rule for session artifact and JSONL integrity mistakes.
npm run audit:harness passes after the repair.
Changes are committed, merged to main, pushed, and task branch cleanup is reported.
validation_plan: Run npm run audit:harness before and after fixes.
Inspect affected JSONL files with a parser after edits.
Review documentation diff for scope and consistency.

risk_assessment.tier_reason: Multiple operational ledgers and guide documents may be edited; no product runtime code or security/authentication logic changes.
risks: Accidentally rewriting historical ledger facts instead of appending correction metadata.
Masking a real process issue without documenting prevention guidance.
